hub_subtitle: From hesitant communicator to confident global business leader

lesson.menu_title: Closing the Deal — Negotiating Price & Terms
lesson.menu_desc: Negotiate price and terms like a pro: counteroffers, concessions, compromise and closing — and ask for what you want with polite, indirect questions (Could you..., Would you mind..., I was wondering if...) — 32 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 13 — Closing the Deal — Negotiating Price & Terms
lesson.title_tag: Professor View — Milton Sayegh | Aula 13 — Closing the Deal | Business English for Luxury Export | Alumni by Better

listenings.text: Hi Milton, it is Diane from Halstead, following up on the necklace collection. I have spoken to my team, and I have to be direct with you. Your markup is a little high for our floor this season, so I would like to make a counteroffer. Could you do twelve percent off on the first order? In return, we are ready to commit to a larger volume, which gives you something solid to plan around. I know that is a real concession to ask for, so I am open to a compromise: perhaps a smaller discount, but spread the payment over three installments. I would rather not reach a deadlock over a few points, because I genuinely want to settle this and start selling your pieces. Have a think, and let me know what works. Talk soon.

Hey team, Milton here with a quick voice note after the call with Diane. I think we can close this one, but we have to negotiate it smartly. She asked for twelve percent off, which is steep, so my plan is a counteroffer of eight, framed politely: I was wondering if eight percent would work if they raise the volume. The leverage is on our side, because the collection is selling well elsewhere, so we do not have to give away the whole margin. I am happy to offer one concession on payment, the three installments, as long as they settle the rest on delivery. Let us avoid a deadlock by keeping the tone warm and the questions soft. If we meet in the middle on the discount, I think we have a fair bargain and a long-term partner. Let me know your thoughts before Friday.
